subject: UX & Research
title: Global vs. Local Navigation
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Global vs. Local Navigation.

hook.text: A shopping mall has a directory at the entrance (global) and aisle signs in each store (local). Websites need both.

buildup.text: Global navigation stays constant across the site: the main menu, logo link home, search bar. It's always there.

discovery.text: Local navigation shows options within the current section: subcategories, related items, and section-specific filters.

twist.text: Some sites only have global navigation. Deep sections feel like dead ends with no way to explore related content.

climax.text: Both layers work together: global tells you where you are in the site, local tells you where you can go next.

punchline.text: Global says where you are. Local says where to go next. Use both.

quiz.question: What is the difference between global and local navigation?
options: Global stays constant across the site; local shows options within the current section
Global navigation is for international users; local is for domestic users
Global uses images; local uses text
